sentence: Subjective:
HPI: Mr. Jackson, a 45-year-old male, presents with right elbow pain that has been present for the last three weeks, exacerbated by playing tennis.
He describes the pain as sharp, stabbing, and aching, rating it an 8/10 with movement and 3/10 at rest.
The pain is localized to the outside part of the elbow and does not radiate to other areas.
He reports taking Tylenol and ibuprofen, which provide temporary relief, but the pain returns once the medication wears off.
Past medical history: Mr. Jackson has a history of type 2 diabetes and mild intermittent asthma.
He has a surgical history of a gallbladder removal 10 years ago.
He has an allergy to penicillin.
Review of systems: No mention of fever or other symptoms.
Current medications: Metformin 500mg twice a day for diabetes, Flovent twice a day, and Albuterol as needed for asthma.
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned.
Physical exam: The examination is largely normal with a few exceptions.
The heart exam reveals a grade 3/6 systolic ejection murmur, unchanged from prior exams.
The musculoskeletal exam reveals moderate lateral epicondylar tenderness of the right elbow, with pain on passive range of motion and mild pain with resisted extension of the right wrist.
There is no palpable joint effusion.
Test Results: Right elbow x-ray shows no acute fracture or bony abnormality, no malalignment, and no joint effusion.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has acute lateral epicondylitis of the right elbow, also known as tennis elbow, likely due to overuse from increased tennis activity.
Problem list: Acute lateral epicondylitis of the right elbow (active); Type 2 diabetes (active); Mild intermittent asthma (active).
Plan:
I advised Mr. Jackson to rest his right arm as much as possible, avoid repetitive stress in that arm, and to ice the right elbow for 20 minutes at least 3 times a day.
I prescribed Ultram 50mg once a day for pain relief.
I also ordered a hemoglobin A1c level, CBC, and chem-12 blood test to check his diabetes control.
I increased his metformin to 1000mg twice a day and instructed him to check his blood sugar twice daily for the next two weeks.
I wrote a prescription for a refill of Albuterol.
I will see him in follow-up in four weeks to reevaluate his elbow and consider additional imaging or physical therapy if needed.
Follow-up: I will see Mr. Jackson in four weeks for a follow-up appointment.
